scenes.speech: Manage data smartly with lists and dictionaries!
scenes.title: Lists and Dictionaries

scenes.speech: A container that holds multiple values!
scenes.title: Basic Lists
scenes.content: fruits = ['apple','orange','banana'] creates a list. Access with fruits[0]='apple'!

scenes.speech: Add, remove, and change items in a list!
scenes.title: List Operations
scenes.content: append() to add, remove() to delete, pop() to take last item, len() to get the length!
scenes.highlight: append add  remove delete  pop take  len length

scenes.speech: Extract a portion of a list!
scenes.title: Slicing
scenes.content: fruits[1:3] gets items 1-2. fruits[:2] gets the first 2. fruits[::2] skips every other!

scenes.title: Basic Dictionaries
scenes.content: person = {'name':'Alice','age':10} manages data with names as keys!

scenes.speech: Add, change, and delete dictionary data!
scenes.title: Dictionary Operations
scenes.content: person['email'] = 'a@a.com' to add, del person['age'] to delete, person.keys() for all keys!

scenes.speech: An immutable list is called a tuple!
scenes.title: Tuples
scenes.content: point = (3, 5) creates a tuple with (). It can't be changed. Use for coordinates and config values!

scenes.title: Sets
scenes.content: colors = {'red','blue','green'} — sets automatically remove duplicates. Union and intersection operations too!

scenes.speech: Pick the right structure for the job!
scenes.title: Choosing Data Structures
scenes.content: Ordered? → List. Lookup by key? → Dict. Immutable? → Tuple. No duplicates? → Set. Choose by purpose!
scenes.highlight: List · Dict · Tuple · Set → choose by purpose!

scenes.speech: You can now use data structures!
scenes.title: Lesson Complete!
